question: [File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: filterKeys.TRANSACTION_TYPE] Can an unprivileged external user supply filterKeys.TRANSACTION_TYPE with an invalid integer value that maps to no known transaction type, causing the API to execute a query that returns httpStatusCodes.NO_CONTENT (204) or httpStatusCodes.BAD_REQUEST (400) in a way that, under high concurrency, exhausts error-handling resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: filterKeys.TRANSACTIONS] Can an unprivileged external user set filterKeys.TRANSACTIONS to 'true' for an account endpoint, causing the API to include all transactions for that account in the response, which for high-volume accounts may be an unbounded result set that exhausts memory and triggers httpStatusCodes.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE (503)?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: requestStartTime / responseBodyLabel] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a slow query that causes the requestStartTime-based elapsed time calculation to overflow or produce a negative value, causing the error logging middleware to log misleading timing data that masks a real network partition event from operators?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Constant: responseCacheKeyLabel] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request whose responseCacheKeyLabel key collides with a legitimate cached response (e.g., by supplying equivalent but differently-encoded parameters), causing the API to serve a cached response for a different query and creating a data integrity partition for affected clients?
